_meta.name: Material Takeoff — Quantity Knowledge Base
_meta.description: Maps a project scope + size to an order-ready material quantity list. Same engine pattern as House Intelligence (a dataset of rules + a lookup engine), pointed at a different question: House Intelligence says what to INSPECT, Material Takeoff says what to BUY. Quantities already include standard waste factors, and the engine also reports the raw measurement + the waste % so the math is transparent and auditable. v1 covers ONE project type: kitchen_remodel.
_meta.disclaimer: This is an order-ready STARTING POINT computed from typical estimating standards, NOT a substitute for field measurement. Cabinets and countertops are MADE-TO-MEASURE: always field-verify before ordering those. Verify all quantities against the actual jobsite before purchasing.
principles: No waste, no shortage: waste factors are applied so you do not run short; coverage rates are deliberately conservative (e.g. thinset 75 sqft/bag, not 90+) so the order does not fall short.
Show the math: every line carries the raw measurement, the waste % (or coverage rate), and the final order quantity so the number is auditable.
Made-to-measure items (cabinets, countertops) carry no blind waste factor and must be field-verified.
sources: Drywall: 4x8 sheet = 32 sqft; waste 10% standard / 15% kitchens (cutouts). Joint compound ~25-30 lb per 100 sqft (Level 4). Tape ~2-3 sqft per linear ft.
Tile waste: 5-7% straight-lay, 10-15% diagonal, 15-20% mosaic/herringbone. One dye lot.
Cabinets: linear feet, made-to-measure (no waste factor), field-verify. 200 sqft kitchen ~= 35-45 LF total (~60% base / ~40% upper); 100 sqft / 10x10 ~= 20 LF.
Countertop: ~1.0 sqft finished per LF of base run; raw slab +15% (solid) to +25% (veined / pattern-matched).
Thinset: ~75-80 sqft per 50 lb bag (1/4 x 1/4 trowel).
Grout: ~100 sqft per 25 lb bag (12in tile, 1/8in joints); smaller tile / mosaic uses more.
_meta.pricing_disclaimer: Prices are LIVE from a third-party Home Depot pricing API at request time and are matched to a search term per quality tier, NOT to your exact chosen SKU. Treat them as a budgetary estimate, not a quote. Labor is a rough rule-of-thumb figure you should override per job. The markup/profit layout is arithmetic on those inputs, not tax/overhead/financial advice.
pricing_sources: Home Depot has NO official public pricing API; live prices come from a third-party service (e.g. SerpApi Home Depot, BigBox) via HOMEDEPOT_API_KEY. No key -> pricing is unavailable (there is no baked price catalog).
Quality tiers: good = builder grade, better = mid-grade, best = premium. Each tier maps to a Home Depot search term per material line.
Labor default = 100% of material cost (a common remodel 1:1 rule of thumb) and is the roughest number here; always override with laborPct or laborCost.
Profit layout shows BOTH the applied markup % (price = cost x (1 + markup)) and the gross margin % it implies (margin = profit / price).

kitchen_remodel.label: Full Kitchen Remodel
kitchen_remodel.summary: Quantified order list for a full kitchen remodel: cabinets, countertop, backsplash + floor tile and setting materials, drywall, and a plumbing / electrical rough-in checklist.

inputs.name: kitchenSqft
inputs.description: Kitchen FLOOR area in square feet. Drives every derived quantity.

inputs.name: ceilingHeight
inputs.description: Wall height, for drywall wall-area calculation.

inputs.name: cabinetLF
inputs.description: Known TOTAL cabinet linear feet (base + upper). If omitted, derived from floor area. If given, split 60% base / 40% upper unless baseCabinetLF / upperCabinetLF are also given.

inputs.name: baseCabinetLF
inputs.description: Known base-cabinet linear feet. Overrides the base split. Also drives countertop and backsplash if those are not given.

inputs.name: upperCabinetLF
inputs.description: Known upper-cabinet linear feet. Overrides the upper split. Drives under-cabinet lighting length.

inputs.name: countertopSqft
inputs.description: Known FINISHED countertop area. If omitted, derived as ~1.0 sqft per LF of base run.

inputs.name: countertopType
inputs.description: Slab waste differs: 'solid' = +15% slab waste, 'veined' = +25% (pattern-matched).

inputs.name: tileLayout
inputs.description: Tile layout for backsplash + floor. Sets the tile waste %: straight 7%, diagonal 15%, herringbone 20%, mosaic 20%.

inputs.name: floorTile
inputs.description: Whether the floor is being tiled. Drives floor tile, and contributes to thinset + grout. Set false for LVP / wood / existing floor.

inputs.name: backsplashHeight
inputs.description: Backsplash height between counter and uppers. 18in is standard; use 0 to skip backsplash.

inputs.name: openings
inputs.description: Number of door/window openings, deducted from drywall wall area (~15 sqft each).

inputs.name: wallPerimeterLF
inputs.description: Known wall perimeter. If omitted, estimated as 4 * sqrt(floor area) (square-room approximation).

inputs.name: includeCeiling
inputs.description: Add the ceiling (= floor area) to the drywall quantity. Default false (most remodels keep the ceiling).

inputs.name: roomShape
inputs.description: Room shape - refines the wall-perimeter estimate (square 4*sqrt(A) up to u_shaped ~5.2*sqrt(A)); 'island' also adds ~15% cabinet/counter run. Ignored when wallPerimeterLF is provided (an exact measurement always wins).

inputs.name: floorTileLayout
inputs.description: Per-surface FLOOR tile layout (sets its waste %). Falls back to tileLayout when omitted.

inputs.name: backsplashTileLayout
inputs.description: Per-surface BACKSPLASH tile layout (sets its waste %). Falls back to tileLayout when omitted.

inputs.name: floorTileBoxSqft
inputs.description: Coverage per box of your chosen FLOOR tile. When given, floor tile is ordered in whole BOXES (pack_round) instead of sqft - and priced per box.

inputs.name: backsplashTileBoxSqft
inputs.description: Coverage per box of your chosen BACKSPLASH tile. When given, backsplash tile is ordered in whole BOXES - and priced per box.

inputs.name: countertopSlabSqft
inputs.description: Usable area per countertop SLAB. When given, countertop is ordered in whole SLABS (pack_round) instead of sqft.

inputs.name: includeDemolition
inputs.description: Add-on (default off): demolition debris volume -> dumpster count.

inputs.name: includeSubfloor
inputs.description: Add-on (default off): subfloor / underlayment panels over the floor area.

inputs.name: includePaint
inputs.description: Add-on (default off): primer + topcoat paint over the drywall surface.

inputs.name: includeTrim
inputs.description: Add-on (default off): baseboard trim around the perimeter, less door openings.

inputs.name: includeHardware
inputs.description: Add-on (default off): cabinet pulls / knobs, derived from cabinet linear feet.

inputs.name: paintCoats
inputs.description: Number of topcoats when includePaint is on (primer is always 1 coat).

geometry.perimeter_note: Shape-aware estimate; override with wallPerimeterLF when the real perimeter is known.
geometry.perimeter_factor_note: Perimeter = factor * sqrt(floor area). Square is the minimum-perimeter rectangle; longer/irregular shapes have more wall per sqft. wallPerimeterLF overrides this entirely. Default 'square' (4.0) preserves the original square-room model.
geometry.island_cabinet_note: An island adds a cabinet/counter run: multiply the floor-area cabinet estimate by this when roomShape is 'island' and cabinetLF is not provided.
geometry.cabinet_lf_per_sqft_note: Calibrated to: 100 sqft -> 20 LF total, 200 sqft -> 40 LF total. Scales with FLOOR AREA (not perimeter) so larger kitchens are not under-counted.

cabinets.note: Linear feet, made-to-measure -> NO waste factor. Always field-verify before ordering.

countertop.note: Raw = finished sqft (~1.0 per base LF). Slab order adds 15% (solid) to 25% (veined) cutting waste. Field-verify.

tile.waste_note: Top of each sourced band, chosen for the no-shortage promise (straight 5-7%, diagonal 10-15%, mosaic/herringbone 15-20%). Order from ONE dye lot.

thinset.note: ~75-80 sqft per 50 lb bag at 1/4 x 1/4 trowel. We use 75 (low end) so the order does not fall short. Applied to the substrate area being set (backsplash + floor).

grout.note: ~100 sqft per 25 lb bag for ~12in tile / 1/8in joints. Mosaic / small tile uses roughly double the grout, so coverage drops to ~50 sqft/bag for the 'mosaic' layout.

drywall.note: 4x8 sheet = 32 sqft. 15% waste for kitchens (cutouts) per standard. Wall area = perimeter * ceiling height, minus openings.

joint_compound.note: ~25-30 lb per 100 sqft (Level 4 finish); we use 30 (high end). Sold as ~4.5-gal (~61.7 lb) ready-mix buckets.

drywall_tape.note: Tape covers ~2-3 sqft per linear ft; we use 2 (more tape) so seams are covered. Sold in 250 ft rolls.

drywall_screws.note: ~32 screws per 4x8 sheet (16in field / 8-12in edges). A 1 lb box of 1-1/4in coarse screws holds ~350.

demolition.note: Debris volume estimate for a full gut (~0.08 cu yd per sqft of floor). Ordered as dumpsters; confirm local haul limits and weight caps.

subfloor.note: 4x8 underlayment / subfloor panels (32 sqft each) with 10% cutting waste.

paint.note: ~350 sqft per gallon per coat. Primer 1 coat + topcoats (default 2), applied to the drywall surface area.

trim.note: Baseboard by perimeter less ~3 ft per opening, +10% for miters/offcuts, sold in 16 ft sticks.

hardware.note: ~0.9 pulls/knobs per cabinet LF (mix of doors and drawer banks). Field-verify the actual door/drawer count.

plumbing.note: Field-verify single vs. double bowl and cabinet fit.
Includes deck plate if required by the sink.
Braided stainless, sized to the shutoffs.
One hot, one cold under the sink.
Air gap or high loop per local code.
Confirm disposal vs. plain basket strainer; size the branch drain.

electrical.note: NEC: every counter >=12in gets a receptacle and no point along the counter is >24in from one. All counter receptacles GFCI-protected. (~1 per 4 LF of base run.)
NEC minimum two 20A small-appliance circuits serving the counter.

electrical.item: Dedicated appliance circuits (dishwasher, disposal, microwave, refrigerator)
electrical.note: One dedicated circuit each; confirm which appliances are present.
240V 40-50A for an electric range; omit if gas.
Size the run to the upper-cabinet linear feet; one switched/dimmed circuit.
Rough estimate at ~30 ft average run per circuit; size 12/2 (20A) and 14/2 (15A) per the actual circuit count and runs.

labor.note: ROUGHEST number in this takeoff. Labor defaults to 1:1 with material cost (a common remodel rule of thumb), NOT an estimate of your crew. Override per job with laborPct (percent of materials) or laborCost (absolute dollars).

search.best: solid wood soft close base cabinet

base_cabinets.note: Priced per linear foot from a representative stock cabinet. Made-to-measure — treat as a rough budget only; field-verify actual cabinet order.

search.best: solid wood soft close wall cabinet

upper_cabinets.note: Priced per linear foot from a representative stock wall cabinet. Made-to-measure — rough budget only.

countertop.note: Priced per finished square foot. Made-to-measure (fabrication + edges vary) — rough budget only.

search.best: premium large format thinset mortar 50 lb
1/2 in mold and moisture resistant drywall 4x8

search.better: lightweight all purpose joint compound bucket

demolition_dumpster.note: Dumpster/haul-away pricing varies hugely by locality - treat as a rough placeholder and use a local quote.

search.best: 3/4 in tongue and groove plywood subfloor

bathroom_remodel.label: Full Bathroom Remodel
bathroom_remodel.summary: Quantified order list for a bathroom remodel: floor + shower/tub surround tile and setting materials, waterproofing, cement backer board, drywall, vanity + top, and a plumbing / electrical rough-in checklist. Scope is configurable (defaults to a full remodel).

inputs.name: bathroomSqft
inputs.description: Bathroom FLOOR area in square feet. Drives floor tile + geometry.

inputs.name: ceilingHeight
inputs.description: Wall height, for drywall + surround wall-area calculation.

inputs.name: showerType
inputs.description: Wet zone: 'tub_shower' (tub with tiled surround), 'shower' (walk-in tiled shower), 'tub' (tub, minimal surround), 'none' (no shower/tub tile). Sets the surround wall area and whether waterproofing/backer board are added.

inputs.name: showerWallSqft
inputs.description: Known tiled surround wall area. If omitted, derived from showerType.

inputs.name: tileLayout
inputs.description: Tile layout for floor + walls. Sets tile waste %: straight 7%, diagonal 15%, herringbone 20%, mosaic 20%.

inputs.name: wainscotHeight
inputs.description: Wall-tile wainscot height around the rest of the room (beyond the wet zone). 0 = none.

inputs.name: vanityLF
inputs.description: Vanity linear feet. Drives the vanity + vanity top. Default 3 (a typical single vanity).

inputs.name: vanityTopType
inputs.description: Vanity top slab waste: 'solid' +15%, 'veined' +25% (pattern-matched).

inputs.name: floorTile
inputs.description: Whether the floor is tiled. Set false for LVP / existing floor.

inputs.name: includeVanity
inputs.description: Include the vanity + vanity top. Set false to keep the existing vanity.

inputs.name: includeWaterproofing
inputs.description: Include a waterproofing membrane over the wet zone. Set false if not waterproofing to a membrane system.

inputs.name: openings
inputs.description: Number of door/window openings, deducted from wall area (~15 sqft each).

inputs.name: includeCeiling
inputs.description: Add the ceiling (= floor area) to the drywall quantity.

inputs.name: wallPerimeterLF
inputs.description: Known wall perimeter. If omitted, estimated from roomShape (4 * sqrt(floor area) for square).

inputs.name: roomShape
inputs.description: Room shape - refines the wall-perimeter estimate (square 4*sqrt(A) up to u_shaped ~5.2*sqrt(A)). Ignored when wallPerimeterLF is provided.

inputs.name: floorTileLayout
inputs.description: Per-surface FLOOR tile layout (sets its waste %). Falls back to tileLayout when omitted.

inputs.name: wallTileLayout
inputs.description: Per-surface WALL/surround tile layout (sets its waste %). Falls back to tileLayout when omitted.

inputs.name: floorTileBoxSqft
inputs.description: Coverage per box of your FLOOR tile. When given, floor tile is ordered in whole BOXES (pack_round) - and priced per box.

inputs.name: wallTileBoxSqft
inputs.description: Coverage per box of your WALL tile. When given, wall tile is ordered in whole BOXES - and priced per box.

inputs.name: vanityTopSlabSqft
inputs.description: Usable area per vanity-top SLAB. When given, the vanity top is ordered in whole SLABS (pack_round) instead of sqft.

inputs.name: includeDemolition
inputs.description: Add-on (default off): demolition debris volume -> dumpster count.

inputs.name: includeSubfloor
inputs.description: Add-on (default off): subfloor / underlayment panels over the floor area.

inputs.name: includePaint
inputs.description: Add-on (default off): primer + topcoat paint over the DRY wall area (tiled wet zone excluded).

inputs.name: includeTrim
inputs.description: Add-on (default off): baseboard trim around the perimeter, less door openings.

inputs.name: includeHardware
inputs.description: Add-on (default off): vanity pulls / knobs, derived from vanity linear feet.

inputs.name: paintCoats
inputs.description: Number of topcoats when includePaint is on (primer is always 1 coat).

geometry.perimeter_factor_note: Perimeter = factor * sqrt(floor area). wallPerimeterLF overrides. Default 'square' (4.0) = the original square-room model.
geometry.shower_wall_note: Default tiled surround area by type; override with showerWallSqft when known. tub_shower ~ three-wall tub surround to ~6 ft; shower ~ full-height walk-in; tub ~ short tub surround.
geometry.shower_floor_note: Tiled shower-pan area (walk-in shower only); tubs come with their own base.

tile.waste_note: Top of each sourced band for the no-shortage promise. Order from ONE dye lot.

thinset.note: ~75 sqft per 50 lb bag (1/4 x 1/4 trowel). Applied to all tiled substrate (floor + walls).

grout.note: ~100 sqft per 25 lb bag (~12in tile). Mosaic/small tile drops to ~50 sqft/bag.

waterproofing.note: Sheet/liquid waterproofing membrane over the wet zone (surround walls + tiled pan). ~175 sqft per roll/kit (conservative).

backer_board.note: 1/2 in cement backer board, 3x5 sheet = 15 sqft, behind wet-wall tile. 10% waste for cuts.

drywall.note: 4x8 sheet = 32 sqft, 15% bathroom waste (cutouts). Dry walls only; wet walls use backer board.

joint_compound.note: ~30 lb per 100 sqft (Level 4). ~4.5-gal (~61.7 lb) ready-mix bucket.

drywall_tape.note: Tape ~2 sqft per LF; 250 ft rolls.

drywall_screws.note: ~32 screws per 4x8 sheet; ~350 per 1 lb box.

vanity.note: Linear feet, made-to-measure -> NO waste factor. Field-verify before ordering.

vanity_top.note: ~1.8 sqft per vanity LF (22in deep). Slab order adds 15% (solid) to 25% (veined) cutting waste. Field-verify.

demolition.note: Bathrooms produce more debris per sqft than kitchens (tile, tub, substrate) - ~0.12 cu yd per sqft. Confirm local haul/weight limits.

subfloor.note: 4x8 underlayment / subfloor panels (32 sqft each) with 10% cutting waste.

paint.note: ~350 sqft per gallon per coat, applied to the DRY wall area only (the tiled wet zone is excluded). Use a bathroom/moisture-rated paint.

trim.note: Baseboard by perimeter less ~3 ft per opening, +10% for miters/offcuts, sold in 16 ft sticks.

hardware.note: ~0.9 pulls/knobs per vanity LF. Field-verify the actual door/drawer count.

plumbing.note: Confirm rough-in (10/12/14 in) and one-piece vs two-piece.
Sink/basin + faucet; add a second for a double vanity.
Braided stainless to the vanity; toilet uses one cold supply.
Two under the vanity (hot/cold) + one for the toilet.
Vanity drain with tailpiece and washers.
Pressure-balanced or thermostatic valve + trim kit. Omit if showerType is none.
Tub, or a shower pan/base + drain assembly for a walk-in.
DWV for toilet, vanity, and shower/tub per local code.

electrical.note: NEC: a dedicated 20A circuit for bathroom receptacles.
Ducted to the outside; size to room volume. Consider fan/light combo.
Vanity/sconce lighting fixture(s); one switched circuit.
Rough estimate ~30 ft per circuit; size 12/2 (20A) per actual runs.

labor.note: ROUGHEST number: labor defaults to 1:1 with material cost. Override with laborPct or laborCost.

search.best: premium large format thinset mortar 50 lb
1/2 in mold and moisture resistant drywall 4x8

search.better: lightweight all purpose joint compound bucket

search.best: solid wood bathroom vanity soft close

demolition_dumpster.note: Dumpster/haul-away pricing varies hugely by locality - rough placeholder; use a local quote.
